creator: genie@geniechka.ru
text:
I made some additional testing and found that this indeed happens on 
upgraded system but not on clean install of 8.4.

Looks like freebsd-update does try to rebuild the password database but does 
not quite succeed, leaving binary files in somewhat corrupted state. As a 
result, after the first installation attempt, we have:
1) seemingly synchronized master.passwd, pwd.db, spwd.db and passwd -- at 
least filetime is the same.
2) correct logcheck records in the text files.
3) some logcheck-related records in binary databases (at quick glance not 
much different from those for normal working users).
4) however, they are evidently incorrect as "pw user show" does not see a 
user, thus installation tries to add it, and this leads to those strange 
errors:
pw: user 'logcheck' already exists
---> Created user "logcheck".
install -d -o logcheck -g logcheck /var/db/logcheck /var/run/logcheck
install: logcheck: Invalid argument

If I run vipw, it regenerates binary password databases correctly and then 
everything works quite nicely.

This may be related to recent discussion of freebsd-update we had, starting 
at
http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-questions/2013-June/251781.html
Unfortunately I no longer have a clean (just upgraded) system to check what 
the database looks like there.

Don't know if a PR for freebsd-update should be submitted.

Anyway, thanks a lot to Greg for his help!
